hypothesis: Candidates already recognized by shared sizing as SPY-relative leaders may treat a next-open upside gap as momentum confirmation instead of overextension, so exempting only those candidates from the existing 1.5% upside gap cancel could recover entry alpha without changing global gap cancel behavior.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: Production-aligned LLM ranking remains outcome-join sparse, so this run tested a deterministic shared sizing context instead of LLM ranking.

mechanism_insight_check.notes: Global upside-gap cancel sweeps and sector/strategy-only gap exceptions were already rejected. This retry used the accepted SPY-relative leader sizing state as the orthogonal discriminator required by the prior rejection notes.

parameters.single_causal_variable: SPY-relative leader upside-gap cancel exception predicate
parameters.variant: if classify_entry_open_cancel returns gap_cancel and signal sizing has spy_relative_leader_risk_on_multiplier_applied > 1.0, allow the entry

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

gate4_basis: No variant exposure appeared in any canonical fixed window, so no Gate 4 materiality criterion could pass.

production_impact.promotion_requirement: If a future variant has exposure and passes Gate 4, implement signal-aware gap classification in production_parity.py and expose the same decision in run.py/backtester.py.

rejection_reason: The SPY-relative leader upside-gap exception had zero eligible gap-cancel candidates across the fixed three-window replay.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry SPY-relative leader upside-gap exceptions without forward evidence that such candidates actually appear.
Do not rerun global CANCEL_GAP_PCT sweeps around 1-5%.
A valid retry needs event/news confirmation or a different state discriminator with nonzero historical or forward exposure.
